---
title: "Changes from the pre-registration"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    grid:
      body-width: 900px
editor: source
---

← [Back to Online Supplement](0_OnlineSupplement.html)

Here, we describe all changes from the [pre-registration](https://osf.io/v8sgf/files/tp8h3) to the manuscript. Note that the pre-registration is available as a PDF in the "files" section of the OSF registration.

It is worth noting some changes to the numbering of research questions from the pre-registration to the manuscript. Pre-registration research question 3 has been moved to the descriptive statistics section. Pre-registration questions 4 and 5 have been merged into manuscript research question 3.

## All Research Questions

### Major Changes

* Participation time points 2, 7, and 14 were dropped from all analyses because not all participants were eligible to participate at these time points.
* To shorten the manuscript and reduce the number of analyses, for research questions 4–5 we focused only on the four adolescence measures and did not analyse the toddlerhood measures.
* The pre-registration did not specify exclusion criteria; we therefore applied the standard TEDS exclusion criteria provided in the online documentation.

## RQ1

### Major Changes

* Instead of fitting multivariate logistic regression models, we used univariate (single outcome) logistic regression with cluster-robust standard errors and Wald tests.
* McFadden's R^2 was added as a measure of prediction accuracy.
* Participation in web tests and questionnaires at the same time point was modelled separately.

### Minor Changes

* We dropped the following variables from the analyses: twin genetic problems (agenpro1/2), mother/father social class (amoclass/afaclass), mother/father has a job (amojob/afajob), mother/father needs special qualification for job (amospq/afaspq).

## RQ2

### Major Changes

* We added baseline maternal education as an outcome variable.
* We dropped anxiety symptoms (GAD-D) as an outcome variable.
* As with RQ1, we omitted Hellinger's distance and SRMR from the models, and compared variances and correlations instead of covariances.
* Outcomes were narrowed to single time points. Where the pre-registration specified each phenotype across multiple ages (e.g., depression at ages 12, 16, 21, and 26), the manuscript analyses each outcome at a single age (Cognitive Y12, GCSE Y16, Depression Y12, Externalising Y12).
* Twin models were fitted separately by sex.

## RQ3

### Major Changes

* Instead of generating time-point-specific weights, we created individualised weights for each variable and pair of variables, as several variables showed uneven missingness even within the same time point.
* IP-weight truncation at the 1st and 99th percentiles was added (not pre-specified).
* We increased the number of imputations from 100 to 250.

### Minor Changes

* Instead of using IP weights fitted to model participation at a given timepoint, we used IP weights fitted to model missingness in each variable or pair of variables.
* We increased the number of imputed datasets to 250 and iterations to 75.
